input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule T.7.1.4 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.7.1.4
            TITLE: all forward facing edges that could contact a pedestrian (wings, end plates, and undertrays)

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.1.4: All forward facing edges that could contact a pedestrian (wings, end plates, and undertrays)
T.7.1.4 All forward facing edges that could contact a pedestrian (wings, end plates, and undertrays) 
must have a minimum radius of 5 mm for all horizontal edges and 3 mm for vertical edges.   
This may be the radius of the edges themselves, or additional permanently attached pieces 
designed to meet this requirement.

final_answer: All forward facing edges that could contact a pedestrian (wings, end plates, and undertrays) must have a minimum radius of 5 mm for all horizontal edges and 3 mm for vertical edges. This may be the radius of the edges themselves, or additional permanently attached pieces designed to meet this requirement.
